XAI3

Maria, Alba

2026-04-28

#install.packages("tidyverse")
#install.packages("randomForest")
#install.packages("pdp")
#install.packages("ggplot2")
# Cargar librerías
library(tidyverse)
library(randomForest)
library(pdp)
library(ggplot2)

# Para que los resultados sean reproducibles
set.seed(123)

#LOAD AND PREPARE BIKE DATASET

# 1. Load the bike rental dataset
bike_data <- read.csv("day.csv")

# 2. Prepare and denormalize variables (IGUAL QUE EN XAI1)
bike_model_data <- bike_data %>%
  mutate(
    # One-hot encoding para la estación
    season_2 = ifelse(season == 2, 1, 0),
    season_3 = ifelse(season == 3, 1, 0),
    season_4 = ifelse(season == 4, 1, 0),
    
    # Variables del clima
    MISTY = ifelse(weathersit == 2, 1, 0),
    RAIN = ifelse(weathersit %in% c(3, 4), 1, 0),
    
    # Desnormalización (MUY IMPORTANTE PARA LOS GRÁFICOS PDP)
    hum = hum * 100,                                
    windspeed = windspeed * 67,                     
    temp = temp * (39 - (-8)) + (-8),               
    
    # Días transcurridos
    days_since_2011 = as.numeric(as.Date(dteday) - as.Date("2011-01-01"))
  ) %>%
  # Ahora sí, seleccionamos solo las variables finales que vamos a usar en el Random Forest
  select(cnt, days_since_2011, temp, hum, windspeed, season_2, season_3, season_4, 
         yr, mnth, holiday, weekday, workingday, MISTY, RAIN)

#TRAIN RANDOM FOREST MODEL

# Train the Random Forest model to predict bike rentals
bike_rf <- randomForest(
  cnt ~ .,              # Predict 'cnt' using all available features
  data = bike_model_data,
  ntree = 500,          # Number of trees in the forest
  importance = TRUE     # Compute variable importance
)

# Display model summary
print(bike_rf)
## 
## Call:
##  randomForest(formula = cnt ~ ., data = bike_model_data, ntree = 500,      importance = TRUE) 
##                Type of random forest: regression
##                      Number of trees: 500
## No. of variables tried at each split: 4
## 
##           Mean of squared residuals: 411353
##                     % Var explained: 89.02
# Display variable importance values
importance(bike_rf)
##                     %IncMSE IncNodePurity
## days_since_2011 39.91038220     950667148
## temp            41.03649263     644871821
## hum             34.28884436     150998687
## windspeed       19.17958735      98564556
## season_2        10.39450209      12174582
## season_3        11.03751881      51367343
## season_4        18.35199564      43290410
## yr              19.60193932     335246202
## mnth            21.55496876     243342263
## holiday          0.05202187       8283007
## weekday         11.76168719      41530666
## workingday       8.28650998      14799452
## MISTY           21.63879504      34454534
## RAIN            17.52797025      51751130
# Plot variable importance
varImpPlot(bike_rf)

#PDP - DAYS SINCE 2011

# Compute the Partial Dependence Plot for 'instant'
pdp_instant <- partial(
  object = bike_rf,
  pred.var = "days_since_2011",        # Variable to analyze
  train = bike_model_data,
  grid.resolution = 50         # Number of points to evaluate
)

# Plot the PDP
plot_instant <- autoplot(pdp_instant) +
  labs(
    title = "PDP - Influence of days since 2011",
    x = "Days since 2011",
    y = "Predicted bike rentals"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# Display the plot
print(plot_instant)

#PDP - TEMPERATURE

# Compute the Partial Dependence Plot for 'temp'
pdp_temp <- partial(
  object = bike_rf,
  pred.var = "temp",          # Variable to analyze
  train = bike_model_data,
  grid.resolution = 50        # Number of evaluation points
)

# Plot the PDP
plot_temp <- autoplot(pdp_temp) +
  labs(
    title = "PDP - Influence of temperature",
    x = "Temperature (ºC)",
    y = "Predicted bike rentals"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# Display the plot
print(plot_temp)

#PDP - HUMIDITY

# Compute the Partial Dependence Plot for 'hum'
pdp_hum <- partial(
  object = bike_rf,
  pred.var = "hum",          # Variable to analyze
  train = bike_model_data,
  grid.resolution = 50       # Number of evaluation points
)

# Plot the PDP
plot_hum <- autoplot(pdp_hum) +
  labs(
    title = "PDP - Influence of humidity",
    x = "Humidity (%)",
    y = "Predicted bike rentals"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# Display the plot
print(plot_hum)

#PDP - WIND SPEED

# Compute the Partial Dependence Plot for 'windspeed'
pdp_wind <- partial(
  object = bike_rf,
  pred.var = "windspeed",    # Variable to analyze
  train = bike_model_data,
  grid.resolution = 50       # Number of evaluation points
)

# Plot the PDP
plot_wind <- autoplot(pdp_wind) +
  labs(
    title = "PDP - Influence of wind speed",
    x = "Wind speed (km/h)",
    y = "Predicted bike rentals"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# Display the plot
print(plot_wind)

#PDP 2D - TEMPERATURE AND HUMIDITY

# Create heatmap (PDP 2D)
pdp_temp_hum <- partial(bike_rf, pred.var = c("temp", "hum"), chull = FALSE)
plot_temp_hum <- ggplot(pdp_temp_hum, aes(x = temp, y = hum, fill = yhat)) +
  geom_tile(width = 2, height = 4) +   # <-- CAMBIAMOS EL TAMAÑO AQUÍ
  labs(
    title = "2D PDP - Temperature and Humidity",
    x = "Temperature (ºC)",            # <-- Aprovechamos para poner las unidades reales
    y = "Humidity (%)",
    fill = "Prediction"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# Display the plot
print(plot_temp_hum)

# Add real data distribution on top of the PDP
plot_temp_hum_density <- ggplot(pdp_temp_hum, aes(x = temp, y = hum, fill = yhat)) +
  geom_tile(width = 2, height = 4) +   
  geom_density_2d(
    data = bike_model_data,
    aes(x = temp, y = hum),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "2D PDP with data distribution",
    x = "Temperature (ºC)",
    y = "Humidity (%)",
    fill = "Prediction"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# Display the plot with density
print(plot_temp_hum_density)

#HOUSE DATA - LOADING AND PREPARATION

# Load the house dataset
house_data <- read.csv("kc_house_data.csv")

# Display the first rows of the dataset
head(house_data)
ABCDEFGHIJ0123456789

<dbl>

<int>

2641410019220141209T00000053800032.252570724220
3563150040020150225T00000018000021.007701000010

5195440051020150218T00000051000032.001680808010
6723755031020140512T000000122500044.50542010193010
6 rows | 1-10 of 22 columns
# Select relevant variables for the model
house_model_data <- house_data %>%
  select(
    price,
    bedrooms,
    bathrooms,
    sqft_living,
    sqft_lot,
    floors,
    yr_built
  )

summary(house_model_data)
##      price            bedrooms        bathrooms      sqft_living   
##  Min.   :  75000   Min.   : 0.000   Min.   :0.000   Min.   :  290  
##  1st Qu.: 321950   1st Qu.: 3.000   1st Qu.:1.750   1st Qu.: 1427  
##  Median : 450000   Median : 3.000   Median :2.250   Median : 1910  
##  Mean   : 540088   Mean   : 3.371   Mean   :2.115   Mean   : 2080  
##  3rd Qu.: 645000   3rd Qu.: 4.000   3rd Qu.:2.500   3rd Qu.: 2550  
##  Max.   :7700000   Max.   :33.000   Max.   :8.000   Max.   :13540  
##     sqft_lot           floors         yr_built   
##  Min.   :    520   Min.   :1.000   Min.   :1900  
##  1st Qu.:   5040   1st Qu.:1.000   1st Qu.:1951  
##  Median :   7618   Median :1.500   Median :1975  
##  Mean   :  15107   Mean   :1.494   Mean   :1971  
##  3rd Qu.:  10688   3rd Qu.:2.000   3rd Qu.:1997  
##  Max.   :1651359   Max.   :3.500   Max.   :2015
set.seed(123) # Fija la aleatoriedad
house_sample <- house_model_data %>%
  sample_n(3000)

house_rf <- randomForest(
  price ~ .,
  data = house_sample,
  ntree = 500,
  importance = TRUE
)

print(house_rf)
## 
## Call:
##  randomForest(formula = price ~ ., data = house_sample, ntree = 500,      importance = TRUE) 
##                Type of random forest: regression
##                      Number of trees: 500
## No. of variables tried at each split: 2
## 
##           Mean of squared residuals: 58059020598
##                     % Var explained: 56.54
importance(house_rf)
##              %IncMSE IncNodePurity
## bedrooms    12.81854  2.158059e+13
## bathrooms   23.22041  7.322781e+13
## sqft_living 59.51594  1.692828e+14
## sqft_lot    19.87702  4.605898e+13
## floors      17.55243  1.411023e+13
## yr_built    42.82163  4.406349e+13
varImpPlot(house_rf)

#PDP — bedrooms

pdp_bedrooms <- partial(
  object = house_rf,
  pred.var = "bedrooms",
  train = house_sample,
  grid.resolution = 20
)

plot_bedrooms <- autoplot(pdp_bedrooms) +
  labs(
    title = "PDP - Influence of bedrooms",
    x = "Number of bedrooms",
    y = "Predicted house price"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(plot_bedrooms)

#PDP - BATHROOMS

pdp_bathrooms <- partial(
  object = house_rf,
  pred.var = "bathrooms",
  train = house_sample,
  grid.resolution = 20
)

plot_bathrooms <- autoplot(pdp_bathrooms) +
  labs(
    title = "PDP - Influence of bathrooms",
    x = "Number of bathrooms",
    y = "Predicted house price"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(plot_bathrooms)

#PDP - SQFT LIVING

pdp_sqft_living <- partial(
  object = house_rf,
  pred.var = "sqft_living",
  train = house_sample,
  grid.resolution = 50
)

plot_sqft_living <- autoplot(pdp_sqft_living) +
  labs(
    title = "PDP - Influence of living area",
    x = "sqft_living",
    y = "Predicted house price"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(plot_sqft_living)

#PDP - FLOORS

pdp_floors <- partial(
  object = house_rf,
  pred.var = "floors",
  train = house_sample,
  grid.resolution = 20
)

plot_floors <- autoplot(pdp_floors) +
  labs(
    title = "PDP - Influence of number of floors",
    x = "Number of floors",
    y = "Predicted house price"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(plot_floors)
